# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Functions with Managed Identity. Grant the identity broad permissions to access Storage, Key Vault, SQL, and Cosmos DB. Use built-in roles with wildcards for flexibility as we add features. This is for our serverless microservices architecture.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources: 
- name: "storageAccount"   # Resource Name must be unique in the subscription scope, e.g., here naming storage account resource with this identifier only once per group and that makes it easier to manage when referring back later on. If multiple resources need same IDs you should use more descriptive names for them
  type: "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts" # Type of the Resource (should be one defined in Azure REST API)   storage accounts have different properties than other types like VM, CDN endpoint etc., hence are not included here by default with ARM template syntax only defining its name and resource ID
  location: "<location>"       # e.g.: eastus2 for production usage of the serverless microservices architecture - it must match in all regions where these resources will be created there if necessary to guarantee smooth operation within Azure cloud environments like here (East US, West Europe etc.)    storageAccounts' location should same as a region
  apiVersion: "2019-06-01"   # Required when you update existing resource. Must match the provider settings of your chosen service e.g., if this is Microsoft Storage then use '2019-06-01' or later in case ARM templates are updated and they might need a more recent version
  kind: "Storage"             # Type to be created (should one defined by Azure REST API)   type of the resource e.g., storage account should not have any data disks, it has only blob containers so must use 'Storage' here in case ARM templates are updated and they might need a more recent version
  properties: {}                # Required when creating new resources - this will include details like permissions on different services you want (e.g., access keys for storage account). The exact structure of these is dependent upon your service requirement, hence it cannot be included here by default with ARM template syntax only defining the necessary parameters
  dependsOn: []                # Specifies that all resources created in this scope should also wait until completed before proceeding to create next resource. No other nested resourced or templates defined for these are allowed at present time as of writing on such scenarios will be considered unsupported by ARM template syntax and must reevaluated based upon service requirements
